title: attribution — realtime broadcast channels
description: Two broadcast channel families for live UI updates. Tenant-level channel for admin dashboards (campaign-rollup + newly-identified subjects); user-level channel for a user's own attribution profile changes (rare — but referrals dashboard shows real-time attribution). Reverb driver (Laravel Reverb) is the default transport; the notifications module handles fanout. NO facility-level channels — attribution is per-tenant + per-subject, no intermediate scope.

channels.name: tenant.{tenantId}.attribution
channels.description: Tenant-wide attribution + campaign stream. Sink for admin dashboards showing incoming attribution activity, campaign-rollup updates, merge/reset admin actions.
channels.notes: Payload is trimmed to non-confidential fields (see data-classes.json). Individual click IDs + IP hashes NEVER broadcast — only aggregated campaign counters.

channels.name: user.{userId}.attribution
channels.description: Per-user attribution stream. Powers the my-attribution live-updates widget in the user dashboard (rare — most users don't need this; referrals module surfaces it for referrer accounts).
channels.notes: Payload trimmed to what the user needs to render their own attribution overview — no click IDs, no ip_hash, no consent snapshot.

notes: AttributionTouchpointCaptured is NEVER broadcast — high-volume event; broadcasting every touchpoint would saturate the Reverb transport. Consumers that need touchpoint-level realtime updates should poll the API or listen server-side via AsTouchpointListener.
AttributionSuppressedByConsent is NEVER broadcast — compliance signal, batched daily via notifications instead.
AttributionUpdated + AttributionLastTouchUpdated are NEVER broadcast — too noisy; downstream consumers rely on the CampaignRollupComputed aggregate instead.
